Persona-Panel Pre-Screen — LUMAS Homepage

Mobile · DE store · 2026-07-08 · control = live www.lumas.de · 4 challengers · 11 personas × 5 pages × 3 repeats = 165 blind cells
SIMULATED PRE-SCREEN — a prediction to prioritize real A/B tests. Not evidence of uplift. Personas scored blinded static screenshots; the control was scored under identical conditions. Direction + confidence tier only — no % uplift, no significance. The real test in the shop's AbTest module decides. A method that keeps failing calibration is retired, not rationalized.
Bottom line: The artwork-and-value-proposition-in-the-fold direction is the signal worth taking to a real test — it lifted engagement most in the cold-prospecting cohorts (≈49% of homepage traffic) that currently bounce. But this cycle's control was handicapped (its live hero is a video, judged here as a frozen still), and both leading deltas sit within their own noise. Treat the ranking as a hypothesis generator, not a winner declaration — and design the real test to remove the confound.

Verdict table (un-blinded, ranked)

VariantWeighted intentΔ vs controlWeight-winningConsistentConfidenceAdversarial outcome
Control (live homepage)7.74———baseline—
Cold-Visitor Value-Prop
One full artwork + explicit value prop ("Signierte & limitierte Kunst-Editionen · ab 119 €") + trust anchors pulled into the fold, replacing the video hero.
 9.69 +1.95 79% ✓ HIGH Refuter: 3× FATAL (video-as-still confound; €799 anchor bounces target cohort; mean carried by warm personas). Brand PASS. Feasibility M.
Fold Browse-Router
Video hero compacted to ~150px; an 8-tile visual browse grid (Bestseller, Neuheiten, themes, price bands) placed in the first viewport.
 8.65 +0.91 54% ✓ MEDIUM Refuter: 2× FATAL (compacted hero lost its image; Δ within noise). Brand PASS w/ cautions. Feasibility M.
Artwork-First Fold
Video hero replaced by a shoppable 2×2 bestseller grid (real products, artist + "ab €" price).
 7.77 +0.03 31% — LOW Not panelled (rank 3). €2.190 lead price anchored cold cohorts as "not for me".
Tappable Entry-Tiles
Hero and every section heading turned into obviously-tappable cards with chevrons (fixes dead-click hotspots).
 7.64 -0.1 13% — LOW Not panelled (rank 4). Slightly below control.

Weighted intent = traffic-weighted mean of each persona's blended add-to-cart + save intent. Δ = challenger − control. Confidence tier reflects weight-winning share and whether Δ clears the control's own spread (noise). Both top challengers were demoted from their raw tier by FATAL refutations — see below.

Fold: control vs top 2 challengers

Control — live homepage (video-still hero)
#1 Cold-Visitor Value-Prop — artwork + "ab 119 €" + trust
#2 Fold Browse-Router — compact hero + 8-tile grid

Per-persona story — Cold-Visitor Value-Prop vs control

Sorted by traffic weight. The lift concentrates in the cold, low-intent cohorts (IQ-display, long-tail, PMax) that show art + entry price instead of a corporate claim; it is flat-to-slightly-down for the collector and the search-first comparison shopper, and it does not rescue the pure Instagram bouncer.

22% Instagram Ad-Tap Bouncer ▲ ATC 0→0.7 · save 2.7→7.3 · scroll 0%→0%
“Oh, that flower picture is actually gorgeous — probably the one from the ad. Then I see "AB € 799" and think, okay, this is a fancy gallery thing, not for me. Pretty though.”
friction: €799 as the first price I see reads as 'not for me' — the 'ab 119 €' below almost rescues it but the 799 lands first
13% Brand-Search Returning Router ▲ ATC 15.7→23.3 · save 36.7→43.3 · scroll 100%→67%
“Gut, die Suche ist direkt oben — das ist alles, was ich brauche. Das Menin-Motiv mit Preis ist hübsch, aber der 10%-Banner für die "erste Bestellung" gilt für mich ohnehin nicht. Ich tippe entweder sofort in die Suche oder schaue kurz, ob es einen direkten Einstieg zu Neuheiten oder Künstlern gibt.”
friction: Der 10%-Neukunden-Banner ist für mich als Bestandskunde totes Gewicht ganz oben — erster Tap ist das X.
13% IQ-Digital Display Drifter ▲ ATC 1→3.7 · save 2.3→28.7 · scroll 0%→100%
“A genuine photograph — flowers, luminous, the kind of thing I'd stop at in an exhibition — not a video still with a slogan. That it named the artist and a price straight away made me take it seriously rather than flick away.”
friction: The 10% discount bar and the repeated 'über 90% bereits verkauft' urgency feels a touch pushy for something positioning itself as fine art — cheapens the tone slightly
10% Curious Meta First-Toucher ▲ ATC 3.3→4.7 · save 36.7→41.3 · scroll 100%→100%
“Okay, this is not a poster shop — the flower artwork looks stunning, and "kleine Formate schon ab 119 €" immediately calms my fear that everything costs thousands. "Signiert & limitiert" plus an Architectural Digest quote and 19 galleries makes it feel like a real gallery, though the €799 next to the hero piece makes me nervous about what things actually cost.”
friction: The hero piece is captioned 'ab € 799' right under a headline promising 'ab 119 €' — mixed signals about whether this shop is for my budget.
9% Direct-Typing Collector ▼ ATC 35.3→34.7 · save 50.3→52.7 · scroll 100%→100%
“The promo bar shouting 10% off my "first order" is instantly not for me — I've bought here, and it faintly cheapens the object. But there's Menin under acrylic, "signierte & limitierte" right there, and a search bar. Fine, I know this place.”
friction: The -10% first-order bar is aimed at a beginner, not a returning collector — mildly off-putting
8% Retargeted & Long-Tail Drifter ▲ ATC 1→4 · save 2.7→19.3 · scroll 0%→100%
“Oh — flowers, actually pretty. It says "ab 119 €" and there's 10% off my first order, so it's not the untouchable gallery thing I braced for. It loaded straight into a real picture instead of some vague slogan, which is more than I expected from a banner tap.”
friction: Landed on a generic homepage, not the specific piece the banner showed me — I'm not going to rebuild that hunt by hand
7% Brand-Search First-Time Researcher ▬ ATC 8→8 · save 55→55 · scroll 100%→100%
“Okay, this looks serious — a real signed, limited edition over the sofa, and crucially it tells me prices right away: "ab €119" for small formats, this Menin piece "ab €799". The Architectural Digest quote and "19 galleries, over 20 years" hit exactly the reassurance I needed. Not a poster shop.”
friction: The 'over 90% of editions already sold' / 'last copies' scarcity messaging is everywhere and starts to feel pushy — nudges me back toward my 'is this just marketing?' fear
5% PMax/Demand-Gen Cold Clicker ▲ ATC 3.3→3.7 · save 17→24 · scroll 67%→100%
“Okay, flowers in a black frame, looks nice actually. But "signed limited editions" and "from €799" right under it — my stomach drops a little, this feels like a fancy gallery. The 10% bar and the little "ab 119 €" line are the only reason I don't just close it.”
friction: Headline says signed limited editions and 'ab €799' which reads expensive, fights against the 119 € line
5% Organic Comparison Shopper ▼ ATC 7→5.7 · save 32.7→30.7 · scroll 100%→100%
“Search is right where I'd reach for it, and the headline answers my exact question — "signiert & limitiert" — instead of just shouting a promo. The full artwork is shown properly, there's a real price (ab €799), and the Architectural Digest line plus "19 galleries, 20 years" tells me this is a real company, not a print farm.”
friction: Repeated 'über 90% bereits verkauft' scarcity banners start reading as pressure/marketing
5% Newsletter-Clicking Existing Customer ▲ ATC 12→14.7 · save 55→59.3 · scroll 100%→100%
“Oh, the Menin flowers — that's lovely, and it's the kind of thing that made me tap the email. The "kleine Formate schon ab 119 €" line reassures me right away that this isn't only the €799 dream pieces. That first-order 10% banner does needle me a bit though — I've bought three times, where's my treat?”
friction: The 10% off FIRST order banner reads as 'not for you' — mildly stings as a returning buyer
3% Meta-Retargeted Waverer ▲ ATC 10→12 · save 49.3→62 · scroll 100%→100%
“Okay, this is the LUMAS I remember — a big Menin floral, "signiert & limitiert", price anchor at €799. It's not my Beatrice Hug, but it's the right world, and the search bar is right there so I can get back to her without starting from zero. The 10% banner... fine, honestly on an €800 piece that's €80, I'll take it, even if it makes me wince a little.”
friction: The Beatrice Hug piece in the bestseller row is priced far above what I was considering (looks like over €2,000) — for a second I panicked that the one I wanted was out of my range, and I'd have to search to confirm mine is still €600–900.

Adversarial panel (top 2 only)

Refuter — Cold-Visitor Value-Prop (3× FATAL, 4× WEAKENS):
  • FATAL Control's hero is a video; the panel judged a frozen still (a half-lit red wall, two backs). The variant is a still by nature and loses nothing in screenshot form — so the delta is best read as "a composed still beat a crippled frame of a video," not "value prop beat no value prop."
  • FATAL "Not the photo from the ad" was applied to control but the variant is also not ad-matched. A real cold-prospecting landing carries its own ad creative, which would rescue control on the exact axis personas punished it for.
  • FATAL The target cold cohort (Instagram bouncer, 22% weight) reads "AB € 799", concludes "not for me", and bounces (scroll off, ATC 0) — the variant fails on its own stated success criterion.
  • WEAKENS HIGH confidence sits on a spread (1.72) 2.2× the control's (0.77): a polarizing, bimodal result, not a robust one. Warm personas (p3/p4) drive the positive mean while crediting the search bar + funnel shared with control.
  • WEAKENS The variant introduced its own friction the panel flagged: "€799 under a headline promising ab 119 € — mixed signals" and "three scarcity blocks feel like sales pressure."
Refuter — Fold Browse-Router (2× FATAL, 4× WEAKENS):
  • FATAL The compacted hero rendered as flat black text-on-black — the video-still is gone entirely. The artifact the panel scored is not the artifact the hypothesis says would ship.
  • FATAL Δ +0.91 sits inside the variant's own spread (1.53); 54% weight-winning is a bare majority; spread doubled vs control — a within-noise coin-flip wearing a rank.
  • WEAKENS 48px framed thumbnails are flattered by a static 2× screenshot; on a real phone they read as grey smudges (the largest cohort said so). The part that survives scrutiny is the text labels — which a plain link list delivers.
  • WEAKENS Adds a third nav path (search + tiles + Edition-Finder); one persona named the "which do I use" hesitation. Does nothing for the 22%-weight bouncer (router sits below the compacted hero).

Brand — both PASS. Cold-Visitor Value-Prop: no blocking violation — artwork uncropped, "ab 119 €" is on-strategy for the DACH affordability lever (story-before-price), consistent du tone, brand-safe CTA and Architectural-Digest social proof. Fold Browse-Router: PASS with cautions — the compacted hero shows zero artwork and 48px thumbnails are near-decorative (Imagery s06), but those trade-offs are exactly what the test measures. The loud promo-bar / "über 90% verkauft" scarcity / "günstige Einstiegspreise" copy is pre-existing live content shared with the control arm, not introduced by either variant — worth a separate brand-tone ticket. Real build must switch prototype fonts to Utile Display / Helvetica Now Display.

Feasibility — both FEASIBLE-M. The cookie-split A/B infrastructure is fully built and reusable: deterministic weighted assignment, sticky 180-day cookie, ?<test>=B QA preview, per-store on/off + split via PIM glossary keys, and order-tagging for conversion analysis (only ab_portfolio_v1 is registered today). The homepage is a single CMS block homepage-main, so both variants are chiefly CMS content-ops plus a ~6-line variant conditional in home.twig and one AbTestConstants entry. Cold-Visitor Value-Prop needs no new data provider (framed-image endpoint + static caption). Fold Browse-Router needs a new tile-grid component + SCSS and correct price-range facet URLs (the prototype's ?preis=unter-300 is invalid; the real catalog uses a price range facet in cents). No new routes, schema, or OMS work.

Method box. Cells: 165/165 completed, 0 dropped, 0 errors — 33 per variant (no clustering). Evidence pack: fresh 2026-07-08, all four sources live (DWH · GA4 · Clarity · Adtribute), no SOURCE DOWN. Personas: 11, critic-APPROVED first pass; All 11 personas derived from a fresh, complete evidence pack (no SOURCE DOWN) — none flagged low-confidence. Aggregation is deterministic (aggregate.mjs); control resolved via blind-map.json. Calibration status: method v1, first calibration rows recorded (prototype/ab-sim/calibration-log.md). Blind backtest of the live ab_portfolio_v1 catalog test predicts Variant A (full catalog) beats Variant B (curated/low-60 hidden) — unanimous (0% weight for B), Δ −2.32 beyond control spread; real result pending BI readout. This homepage cycle spawns real test ab_homepage_hero_v1, whose result fills the second calibration row.
